codeReview.summary: The commit adds solid E2EE key management primitives and comprehensive unit tests, but it does not wire bootstrap into actual initialization/authenticated-device flows or runtime command paths. As delivered, expected behavior for per-device provisioning and pre-bootstrap command blocking is not enforced in product flow.

issues.description: `mors init` still provisions only identity + DB artifacts and never calls `generateDeviceKeys`/`persistDeviceKeys` (or `getDeviceKeysDir`) to bootstrap E2EE device keys. This means the feature requirement that each authenticated device provisions and persists encryption identity is not actually satisfied by the runtime bootstrap path.

Guard helpers are implemented but not integrated into any CLI/relay secure messaging path in this commit (no callsites outside `src/e2ee/*` and tests). Therefore secure messaging commands are not actually blocked by runtime wiring prior to bootstrap completion.

User remediation text says to run `mors init` to generate device encryption keys, but this commit does not update init to perform E2EE device-key bootstrap, so the guidance is inaccurate at this point in history.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `mors-global-worker` requires manual CLI/API/SSE sanity checks, but this worker handoff reports only automated commands while `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` is true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/mors-global-worker/SKILL.md step 8 vs handoff 2026-02-28T11-21-19-123Z__e2ee-device-bootstrap-and-keystore-foundation__f1765207-4ed5-4e8e-9a22-413ae925df00.json `verification.commandsRun` (no manual/interactive checks listed).

summary: Review failed. Core key-generation/persistence primitives are good, but feature-level runtime integration is incomplete: init/auth flow does not provision device E2EE keys and secure command gating is not wired into product paths in this commit.
